# Index Funds

An index fund is an investment fund that simply tries to match a market index, such
as a broad basket of large companies, rather than picking individual winners. Because
it follows a fixed rule instead of paying analysts to trade actively, it charges very
low fees and spreads money across many holdings at once.

## Why low cost wins

Over long periods, most actively managed funds fail to beat their benchmark after
fees, so the index fund's low cost and broad diversification often leave investors
ahead. Diversification reduces the risk that any single company sinks the portfolio.
The trade-off is that an index fund will never beat the market; by design it accepts
the average return in exchange for low cost and simplicity.
